item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
honestly, there's some legitimately good stuff in the testing logs, but until the myriad of formatting/grammar issues are fixed, and the conprocs and description are given some sort of hook, this is going to have to be a downvote for me.

I do hope you work on improving this though because this has some real potential.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm happy to have helped, but you should have sought more critique first. Two people isn't enough to spot all the weaknesses of a draft :V

+1 regardless

EDIT: I know this is months old, and I know it's very likely that this edit will go unnoticed, but looking back on this SCP made me realize how much potential was untapped. I loved the Joseph I saw during the drafting phase and knew that he could get better with SPaG edits and deeper characterization. The end result ended up being too rushed for its own good and I still feel like the fourth testing log was too long and too bloated. Looking back on this SCP, I will now change my vote to a downvote. So much untapped potential, and so much really, really good shit that's muddled and unpolished.

I do apologize if you come back to read this and it comes across as dickish. I just can't overcome my disappointment with this piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks so much, I'm working on minor reforms in the description as we speak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are some small SPaG edits I would make, and I agree that less time should be spent in the conprocs or the description… but the logs sorta unpolished gold.

So +1 despite the rougher stuff. I recommend you get more critique when posting in the future. Feel free to pm for draft crit if you need a pair of eyes on something.

item.title: Re: Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded: Thank you for your consideration, and I'm glad you enjoyed the final testing logs nonetheless.

item.title: Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded:
Hello, Author. This is a strange one for me (which, hey, is half the point!)

With regards to the technical, the way you handle your footnotes is 'probably fine' in the scope of what a footnote is intended to do but they read less like reference or commentary or citation and more like you just shaved a sentence from a paragraph and made it a footnote. I am not finding a lot of purpose to them other than to convey borderline excessive details and minutia of function. I've no problem with cleaving the things from footnotes that you have but I am left feeling they add very little. Perhaps consider the necessity of each.

Beyond that, I find that the ConProcs and Description actually foreshadow very little about your article in a way that is somewhat harmful. If I am not instantly hooked by the idea of teleportation there is very little here for me to grab ahold of despite the length of your description. That, when added to the daunting size of the test logs, is a bit bewildering.

Having said that, arriving to the last few logs is much more of the surrealistic nightmarish weird type of stuff I want! We just take a really long time to get there.

All in all, I'd like to see this spend less time in establishment so the impact can concentrate. I am a no-vote.
